findings.evidence: `Comap.lean:45` requires `[AddCommGroup C]`; existing API uses `[AddCommMonoid C]` at `Comodule.lean:45-46` and `Subcomodule.lean:44-46`; nearby `Comodule/Preadditive.lean:206-218` derives `AddCommGroup C` locally, and `mathlib/RingTheory/Flat/Basic.lean:340-343` shows the exactness theorem needs additive groups only at the proof step.
findings.fix: Change the file-level carrier assumption to `[AddCommMonoid C] [Module R C] [Coalgebra R C] [Module.Flat R C]`, and add local `letI : AddCommGroup C := Module.addCommMonoidToAddCommGroup R` only in the private proof path that calls group-level exactness.
findings.issue: All public declarations in this file inherit `[AddCommGroup C]`, while `Comodule` and `Subcomodule` are defined with only `[AddCommMonoid C]`; over `[CommRing R]` the group structure on `C` is derivable, so this unnecessarily excludes users of the existing monoid-level comodule API.

summary: The new comap/kernel API is otherwise at the expected level, but its public signatures still expose a stronger coalgebra-carrier assumption than the surrounding comodule API requires over a ring.
